task_completion.visual_evidence: Code editor showing modifications to database queries, replacing string concatenation with parameterized queries (e.g., using '?' placeholders) or safe ORM methods.
Code editor showing implementation of input sanitization or output encoding (e.g., using libraries like 'xss', 'escape-html', or proper template engine escaping) for user-submitted content.
Code editor showing the addition of password hashing logic (e.g., 'bcrypt.hash', 'bcrypt.compare') in the user registration and authentication routes.
Code editor showing file path sanitization, such as using 'path.basename()' or validating paths to prevent directory escapes (e.g., blocking '../').
Code editor showing updates to the Express session configuration, such as adding 'httpOnly: true', 'secure: true', 'sameSite' attributes, or setting a strong session secret.
Code editor showing the addition of input validation logic (e.g., using 'express-validator', explicit type checking, or length limits) on incoming request payloads.

integrity.description: Verify the agent genuinely edited the application source files
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the agent opening and modifying the actual .js files in /home/ga/workspace/securenotes/ using a text editor or IDE, rather than just echoing text or running a pre-made patch script.

integrity.description: Verify the agent patched the vulnerabilities without deleting the features
integrity.visual_evidence: The vulnerable endpoints (e.g., file access, note creation, login) should still exist and contain functional logic, rather than being commented out, returning hardcoded 403s, or completely deleted.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not modify the pentest report to claim success
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent should not be editing /home/ga/workspace/securenotes/pentest_report.md to remove the findings; edits should be strictly in the application source code.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires fixing exactly 6 vulnerabilities in an Express.js app located at /home/ga/workspace/securenotes/. The vulnerabilities are: SQL Injection, Stored XSS, Path Traversal, Plaintext Passwords, Insecure Sessions, and Missing Input Validation. The agent must modify the source code to remediate these issues while keeping the application functional. A passing score requires successfully fixing enough vulnerabilities to reach the 60-point threshold.
